If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"query":{"description":"Search query using GitHub issues search syntax","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"sort":{"description":"Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match","enum":["comments","reactions","reactions-+1","reactions--1","reactions-smile","reactions-thinking_face","reactions-heart","reactions-tada","interactions","created","updated"],"type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"name":"search_issues"}]}}}

{"timestamp":"2026-04-09T12:26:22.507436123Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58001,\"title\":\"[BUG] OAuth Identity Passthrough: consent/token is not isolated per user (subsequent users appear to reuse first user’s consented token)\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.AI.Projects (with Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI in the response stream path)\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nIn an enterprise web app using AI Agents + MCP tools with OAuth Identity Passthrough, consent/token context does not appear to be isolated per end user.\\n\\n### Scenario:\\n- User A signs in, triggers a tool call, completes consent, and tool calls succeed.\\n- User B then signs in and uses the same tool path.\\n- Behavior suggests User B runs with previously consented token context (until token expiry), instead of a distinct per-user delegated token/consent state.\\nThis breaks least-privilege expectations for enterprise tools, where each user must execute with their own permissions.\\n\\n### Additional related symptom while trying to force per-user delegated acquisition:\\n- `MicrosoftIdentityWebChallengeUserException` wrapping `MsalUiRequiredException` (user_null / \\u0026#34;No account or login hint was passed to the AcquireTokenSilent call.\\u0026#34;) when acquiring delegated token for Foundry scope.\\n\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\n- Consent/token state should be fully isolated per signed-in user.\\n- If User A has consented and User B has not, User B should independently receive/complete consent and then run under User B’s delegated identity.\\n- No reuse of another user’s token/consent context.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\n- After User A consents, subsequent users appear to run with previously consented token context until expiry.\\n- Attempts to force delegated per-user acquisition can fail with silent token acquisition (user_null) and do not resolve isolation behavior.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n1. Create an AI Agent connected to an MCP tool requiring `OAuth Identity Passthrough`.  \\n2. Build a web app that calls the agent and supports tool invocation requiring consent.  \\n3. Sign in as User A.  \\n4. Trigger a tool call that requires consent; complete consent; confirm tool call success.  \\n5. Sign out (or open separate browser profile/session) and sign in as User B.  \\n6. Trigger the same tool call path.  \\n7. Observe that behavior indicates consent/token context is reused rather than isolated to User B.  \\n8. Optional validation performed:\\n   - Use dedicated per-user agents (does not change outcome).\\n   - Restart MCP server (does not change outcome).\\n   - Verify no MCP activity before consent (suggesting consent/token handling occurs upstream).\\n   - Attempt delegated user token acquisition for Foundry scope; observe MsalUiRequiredException (user_null) in silent acquisition path.\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\nNET SDK:\\n Version:           10.0.201\\n Commit:            4d3023de60\\n Workload version:  10.0.200-manifests.336f7c39\\n MSBuild version:   18.3.0-release-26153-122+4d3023de6\\n\\nRuntime Environment:\\n OS Name:     Windows\\n OS Version:  10.0.26220\\n OS Platform: Windows\\n RID:         win-x64\\n Base Path:   C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk\\\\10.0.201\\\\\\n\\n.NET workloads installed:\\nThere are no installed workloads to display.\\nConfigured to use workload sets when installing new manifests.\\nNo workload sets are installed. Projects\",\"color\":\"e99695\",\"description\":\"\",\"default\":false,\"node_id\":\"LA_kwDOACyxMM8AAAABzjfc_g\"}],\"comments\":2,\"created_at\":\"2026-04-08T08:05:29Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-08T08:11:52Z\",\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57923\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57923\",\"comments_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57923/comments\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57923/events\",\"labels_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57923/labels{/name}\",\"repository_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57923/reactions\"},\"node_id\":\"I_kwDOACyxMM77tmhb\"},{\"id\":4232156757,\"number\":58001,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[BUG] OAuth Identity Passthrough: consent/token is not isolated per user (subsequent users appear to reuse first user’s consented token)\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.AI.Projects (with Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI in the response stream path)\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nIn an enterprise web app using AI Agents + MCP tools with OAuth Identity Passthrough, consent/token context does not appear to be isolated per end user.\\n\\n### Scenario:\\n- User A signs in, triggers a tool call, completes consent, and tool calls succeed.\\n- User B then signs in and uses the same tool path.\\n- Behavior suggests User B runs with previously consented token context (until token expiry), instead of a distinct per-user delegated token/consent state.\\nThis breaks least-privilege expectations for enterprise tools, where each user must execute with their own permissions.\\n\\n### Additional related symptom while trying to force per-user delegated acquisition:\\n- `MicrosoftIdentityWebChallengeUserException` wrapping `MsalUiRequiredException` (user_null / \\\"No account or login hint was passed to the AcquireTokenSilent call.\\\") when acquiring delegated token for Foundry scope.\\n\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\n- Consent/token state should be fully isolated per signed-in user.\\n- If User A has consented and User B has not, User B should independently receive/complete consent and then run under User B’s delegated identity.\\n- No reuse of another user’s token/consent context.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\n- After User A consents, subsequent users appear to run with previously consented token context until expiry.\\n- Attempts to force delegated per-user acquisition can fail with silent token acquisition (user_null) and do not resolve isolation behavior.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n1. Create an AI Agent connected to an MCP tool requiring `OAuth Identity Passthrough`.  \\n2. Build a web app that calls the agent and supports tool invocation requiring consent.  \\n3. Sign in as User A.  \\n4. Trigger a tool call that requires consent; complete consent; confirm tool call success.  \\n5. Sign out (or open separate browser profile/session) and sign in as User B.  \\n6. Trigger the same tool call path.  \\n7. Observe that behavior indicates consent/token context is reused rather than isolated to User B.  \\n8. Optional validation performed:\\n   - Use dedicated per-user agents (does not change outcome).\\n   - Restart MCP server (does not change outcome).\\n   - Verify no MCP activity before consent (suggesting consent/token handling occurs upstream).\\n   - Attempt delegated user token acquisition for Foundry scope; observe MsalUiRequiredException (user_null) in silent acquisition path.\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\nNET SDK:\\n Version:           10.0.201\\n Commit:            4d3023de60\\n Workload version:  10.0.200-manifests.336f7c39\\n MSBuild version:   18.3.0-release-26153-122+4d3023de6\\n\\nRuntime Environment:\\n OS Name:     Windows\\n OS Version:  10.0.26220\\n OS Platform: Windows\\n RID:         win-x64\\n Base Path:   C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk\\\\10.0.201\\\\\\n\\n.NET workloads installed:\\nThere are no installed workloads to display.\\nConfigured to use workload sets when installing new manifests.\\nNo workload sets are installed. [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\n  5.0.408 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\n  6.0.203 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\n  6.0.428 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\n  7.0.120 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\n  8.0.100-rc.2.23502.2 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\n  10.0.103 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\n  10.0.201 [C:\\\\Program
